Quick Facts
  • Specialty: Men's Downhill
  • Son of Mary and Stan Bennett
  • Began skiing at age 2
  • Comes from a family that has skiing in their blood
  • His mother worked at Alpine Meadows Resort and Dad was a former telemark racer
  • Used to be a BMX racer
  • Hobbies include spending time with his family, golfing, mountain biking, NASCAR and fishing
Biographical Information

Growing up at Palisades Tahoe, Bryce Bennett began skiing at age 2 and developed his racing style through countless powder days and freeriding adventures. By age 8, he had decided he wanted to compete in the Olympics. After progressing through the Palisades Tahoe Mighty Mite program and ski team, Bennett joined the U.S. Ski Team in 2011 following a spring tryout camp. In December 2015, he announced his arrival on the world stage by finishing sixth at a world cup downhill in Val Gardena—his first top-10 result. He returned to that same Italian course in December 2021 to capture his first world cup victory, then replicated that triumph in December 2023, this time starting from bib 34 to stun the field and defeat the reigning world cup downhill champion by three-hundredths of a second. The 2023-24 season marked his best campaign to date, finishing fifth in the FIS World Cup downhill standings.

Bennett’s towering height presents unique advantages and challenges. While cramped start gates at venues like Wengen can be comically tight, his long legs enable him to brilliantly absorb varied terrain and maintain speed through technical sections. His affable personality and self-deprecating humor have made him a fan favorite, whether he's discussing fishing adventures or his latest CrossFit workouts. 

Off the slopes, Bennett embraces a life beyond ski racing. An avid outdoorsman, he spends his off-season working on his house in Tahoe, fishing on Lake Tahoe and mountain biking on local trails. He welcomed a daughter, Kate, in 2025. Fatherhood has added a new perspective to his career as he balances training blocks in South America and Europe with time at home.

Olympic Experience
  • 3-time Olympian
    • Olympic Games Milan 2026, 13th (Downhill - Men)
    • Olympic Games Beijing 2022, 17th (Super G - Men), 19th (Downhill - Men)
    • Olympic Games Pyeongchang 2018, 16th (Downhill - Men), 17th (Super Combined - Men)
World Championships Experience

  • Years of participation: Downhill - Men 2017, 2019, 2021, 2025; Super G - Men 2019, 2021, 2025; Team Alpine Combined - Men 2025; Super Combined - Men 2017, 2019, 2021
  • Top finish: 9th – 2019 (Downhill - Men)

bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Began shooting archery at 7 years old
  • Was in a car accident at the age of 15 that cost him his right arm
  • Married to his wife, Rachel, and has a son, Logan, and a daughter, Natalie
  • Was encouraged by his father to give archery another try after nearly 10 years away from the sport
  • The first time he shot a bow after losing his arm, he used a bow modified to hold with his feet
  • Is a high school physics and engineering teacher, and taught Olympic archer Brady Ellison
  • Made his first world team in 2007, and went on to become the first American to compete in two different disciplines (Compound and Recurve) at two different Paralympic Games
  • Is the first American to win a World Championship in the Para Recurve division
  • Hobbies include fishing, camping, and movies.
Paralympic Experience
  • 5-time Paralympian
    • Paralympic Games Paris 2024, 17th (Individual Recurve Open - Men)
    • Paralympic Games Tokyo 2020, 9th (Individual Recurve Open - Men, Team Recurve Open - Mixed)
    • Paralympic Games Rio de Janeiro 2016, 8th (Individual Recurve Open - Men)
    • Paralympic Games London 2012, 4th (Individual Recurve Standing - Men)
    • Paralympic Games Beijing 2008, 9th (Individual Compound Open - Men)
World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2023 – 9th (Team Recurve Open - Men), 33th (Individual Recurve Open - Men)
  • Years of participation: Individual Recurve Open - Men 2015, 2019, 2023; Team Recurve Open - Men 2015, 2019, 2023; Individual Compound Open - Men 2007, 2011; Team Compound Open - Men 2007, 2011
  • Medals: 3 (2 gold, 1 silver)
    • Gold – 2019 (Team Recurve Open - Men); 2015 (Individual Recurve Open - Men)
    • Silver – 2019 (Individual Recurve Open - Men)

More
Sidney is the oldest daughter of Steve and Jamie Bennion. She has a younger sister and brother. Sidney was first exposed to XC skiing through her elementary school when she was in 5th grade. She loved it so much, that she chose to join the local xc ski club, Team Soldier Hollow, the following winter and stopped competing in moguls and freestyle skiing. Sidney began biathlon in 2018 and continues to be on Team Soldier Hollow for biathlon and trains with Park City Ski and Snowboard for cross country. When Sidney is not training, you can find her charging down the slopes at our local ski areas. Sidney also excels at mountain bike racing and golf and participates on both of these teams affiliated with her high school.

bio.education: Glacier Peak High School (Snohomish) '14 Pikes Peak Community College
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Beranbaum began swimming at age 13 in Everett, Washington. She was part of a musical theatre, but grew tired of it and switched to soccer. Her mother thought a fast-moving game with a hard ball was not a good idea, and persuaded her daughter to pursue swimming. She is a resident athlete at the U.S. Olympic Training Center in Colorado Springs, Colorado. Within her first three years of competitive swimming, she broke U.S. Paralympic records in the 200m free, 50m breast, 200m breast, 100m IM and 200m IM. At the Guadalajara 2011 Parapan American Games in, Beranbaum won gold in the women’s 200m IM and bronze in the 50m fly. Beranbaum was born with achondroplasia and has spoken at schools to spread awareness of achondroplasia
  • Daughter of Michael and Frances
  • Has one sister, Elyse
  • Her grandmother Rose Levy Beranbaum is an author of cookery books.
